goal: Make Foreman production-ready E2E with layered agents (CEO/CTO/eng) and DoDs at every level.
summary: full C-suite org: CPO + CTO + CISO + SRE + QA -> merge -> CEO verify
acceptanceCriteria: CEO DoD #1: Public URLs healthy on a cold cellular phone -- https://foreman-phi.vercel.app loads, /api/health returns 200 ok, Render /health returns 200 ok, /api/voice/config reports liveAvailable=true.

CEO DoD #2: End-to-end customer walk works from a fresh phone: consent -> Start job -> camera frame analysed -> microphone phrase transcribed -> Stop job -> non-fallback session summary returned -> row counts > 0 for sessions/frames/coaching_events/transcript_segments in Supabase.

CEO DoD #3: Security posture is clean: no committed secrets in current HEAD or last 50 commits (gitleaks reports zero leaks), production boot refuses to start without FOREMAN_API_KEY/CORS_ORIGINS, no NEXT_PUBLIC_* AI keys, CORS allowlist strict, rate limits effective on AI routes, Vercel /api/* origin gate active.

CEO DoD #4: Privacy + consent UX clear -- the web client gates capture behind an explicit consent step, a visible recording indicator appears while a session is active, and the consent copy points at the AU privacy / sensitive-data treatment described in CLAUDE.md.

CEO DoD #5: Cost guards documented and enforced -- per-route rate limits on /analyse, /transcribe, /voice/*, frame size cap enforced server-side (>= existing client compression target), and an operator-facing note in DEPLOY.md/SECURITY.md spells out billing alert setup for Anthropic + OpenAI.

CEO DoD #6: Observability minimum -- /ready endpoint exposes anthropic/openai/supabase availability booleans, every 5xx response includes a server-side correlation id in logs, the Fastify logger emits structured JSON in production, and DEPLOY.md documents where to read Render + Vercel logs.

CEO DoD #7: Tests + readiness scripts pass -- `npm run build` in monorepo is clean, `npm test` passes in backend and web workspaces, `bash scripts/check-ready.sh` reports green when env is set, and there is at least one E2E smoke script that walks /sessions/start -> /analyse -> /transcribe -> /sessions/:id/stop against a configurable BASE_URL.

CEO DoD #8: Docs match reality -- README, DEPLOY.md, PHONE_DEMO.md, SECURITY.md, YOUR_ACTIONS.md state only claims backed by code (or explicitly mark them TODO), Render/Vercel URLs are correct, and the Render free-tier cold-start caveat is called out where it matters.

CEO DoD #9: iOS / glasses path scaffold is not regressed -- `native/ios` opens via xcodegen and references the DAT 0.7 SDK as the conventions document; remaining gaps are listed in a follow-up file rather than failing the build.

tasks.name: cpo-product-ux
tasks.scopedGoal: You are the CPO. Own CEO DoD #2 (full phone end-to-end customer walk) and #4 (privacy + consent UX). Decompose into 2-4 broad workers + a verify-cpo-product-ux verifier. Suggested workers: (W1) `consent-and-recording-ux` = explicit consent gate before any capture in web/components/camera-coach.tsx + a visible recording indicator while a session is active + consent copy that points at the AU privacy / sensitive-data treatment in CLAUDE.md; (W2) `phone-walk-polish` = the full Start-job -> frame analyse -> mic transcribe -> Stop-job -> non-fallback summary loop renders correctly on iPhone Safari, including error/empty states, with a screen-recording artifact captured for the handoff; (W3) `demo-script-truth-sync` = PHONE_DEMO.md and YOUR_ACTIONS.md reflect the actual UI on this branch (selector text, button labels, expected status badges). Each child task MUST carry its own engineering DoD in `acceptance`. Your subplan's `acceptanceCriteria` MUST include: (i) consent gate cannot be bypassed (covered by a web test), (ii) recording indicator visible during an active session (covered by a web test), (iii) consent copy mentions AU privacy / sensitive-data handling, (iv) PHONE_DEMO.md and YOUR_ACTIONS.md updated to match the UI exactly, (v) all CPO-owned tests pass via `cd web && npm test`, (vi) `npm run build --workspace web` clean. SHIP-FAST: commit and push a minimal plan.json within the first 10 minutes of your run, then spend the rest of your runtime in `bun cli.ts run` waiting on children. MODEL CONSTRAINTS: every child you publish MUST set `tasks[].model` to one of `claude-opus-4-7`, `gpt-5.3-codex-high-fast`, or `composer-2-fast` (others are rejected by this CURSOR_API_KEY). Do NOT open PRs from inside the subtree; the root opens the PR. If a CEO DoD bullet is blocked on operator-owned action (e.g. third-party secret), list it under `## Notes` -- do not fail the subplanner on operator-only items.
acceptance: CEO DoD #2 evidenced: web test for the full session walk happy path (or a documented manual recording artifact in the handoff).
CEO DoD #4 evidenced: consent gate cannot be bypassed; recording indicator renders during an active session; consent copy references AU privacy / sensitive-data handling.
`cd web && npm test` passes on your handoff branch.
`npm run build --workspace web` is clean on your handoff branch.
PHONE_DEMO.md and YOUR_ACTIONS.md updated to match the UI on this branch (no aspirational claims).
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `git checkout <cpo-product-ux branch>` and `npm install`.

## Automated
- `npm run build --workspace web` (clean).
- `cd web && npm test` (passes).

## Manual
- Open `http://localhost:3000` after `npm run dev:web`. Confirm: consent gate blocks capture until explicitly accepted; once accepted, a recording indicator is visible while the session is active; consent copy mentions AU privacy / sensitive-data handling.
- Read PHONE_DEMO.md + YOUR_ACTIONS.md end-to-end; spot-check each claim against the UI on this branch.

## Gotchas
- Worker must capture a screen recording of the consent flow + active session and reference the artifact path in its handoff (per worker prompt quality floor).

tasks.name: cto-engineering
tasks.scopedGoal: You are the CTO. Own CEO DoD #9 (iOS / glasses path scaffold not regressed) plus the technical backbone NOT owned by the other C-suite siblings. Specifically: backend non-security business logic (analyse, transcribe, summarise, voice, db, prompts, routes other than /ready), shared zod schemas + FrameSource interface, web client platform code (web/lib/**, web/app/api/**, web/next.config.mjs), and the iOS scaffold. You DO NOT own: security-sensitive auth/session/validate code (CISO), /ready or structured logging (SRE), test/smoke scripts (QA), consent UX (CPO), docs (docs-truth-sync). Decompose into 2-4 broad workers (resist further fan-out). Suggested clustering: (W1) `vp-backend-business-logic` = backend AI-route hardening (analyse, transcribe, voice, summarise) with error-path tests + payload size enforcement coordinated with CISO via shared limits in config.ts; (W2) `vp-web-platform` = web/lib + web/app/api error boundaries, retry, `same-origin` proxy correctness, type-clean; (W3) `vp-native-mobile` = `xcodegen generate` in native/ios sanity-check + DAT 0.7 reference confirmation + gap list appended to native/ios/README.md if xcodebuild is unavailable in CI. Each child MUST carry its own engineering DoD in `acceptance`. Pair behaviour-changing workers with verifiers. End your subtree with a `merge-cto-deliverable` worker that consolidates onto a single branch + a `verify-cto-engineering` verifier. SHIP-FAST: commit and push a minimal plan.json within the first 10 minutes of your run. MODEL CONSTRAINTS: every child MUST set `tasks[].model` to one of `claude-opus-4-7`, `gpt-5.3-codex-high-fast`, or `composer-2-fast`. Do NOT open PRs from inside the subtree. If a CEO DoD bullet is blocked on operator-owned action, list it under `## Notes` -- do not fail.
acceptance: CEO DoD #9 evidenced: `cd native/ios && xcodegen generate` succeeds on your handoff branch (if `xcodegen` is unavailable in CI, document the gap in native/ios/README.md and mark this item as satisfied-with-doc-gap in your handoff).
Backend AI routes (`analyse`, `transcribe`, `voice`, `sessions`) have error-path tests in backend/src that pass via `cd backend && npm test`.
Web platform code (web/lib, web/app/api) builds clean via `npm run build --workspace web` and passes `cd web && npm test`.
Shared `@foreman/shared` builds clean and is consumed unchanged by backend + web.
No path in `pathsForbidden` was modified on your handoff branch (verify via `git diff --name-only main..<your-branch>`).
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `git checkout <cto-engineering branch>` and `npm install`.

## Automated
- `npm run build` (clean).
- `cd backend && npm test` (passes).
- `cd web && npm test` (passes).
- `cd native/ios && xcodegen generate` (passes; if `xcodegen` missing on the host, the verifier confirms native/ios/README.md documents the gap).

## Manual
- `git diff --name-only main..<your-branch>` -> no file from `pathsForbidden` appears.

tasks.name: ciso-security
tasks.scopedGoal: You are the CISO. Own CEO DoD #3 (security posture is clean). NOTE: two prior subplanner attempts at this scope died mid-plan-draft -- you are now a direct worker so you can spend ALL your runtime DOING the work instead of decomposing it. Tackle these four security slices in priority order; commit + push as you go so partial progress is preserved: (1) GITLEAKS AUDIT + CI -- run gitleaks against current HEAD + last 50 commits (install via `curl -sSfL https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks/releases/download/v8.18.4/gitleaks_8.18.4_linux_x64.tar.gz | tar -xz -C /tmp && sudo mv /tmp/gitleaks /usr/local/bin/`), fix or document any high-severity finding, add `.github/workflows/gitleaks.yml` if missing (must run on push + pull_request), add `.gitleaks.toml` baseline if helpful. (2) BOOT-AND-RUNTIME GUARDS TESTS -- write `backend/src/assert-production-security.test.ts` that asserts the backend refuses to start (throws or process.exits non-zero) when `NODE_ENV=production` and either `FOREMAN_API_KEY` or `CORS_ORIGINS` is missing; the test should also confirm `CORS_ORIGINS` defaults are strict in production. (3) VERCEL ORIGIN GATE TEST -- add or improve a test that exercises `web/middleware.ts` to confirm /api/* calls without a valid app origin/referer are blocked. (4) RATE-LIMIT + NO-CLIENT-SECRETS PROOF -- add integration tests in backend/src that hit /analyse and /transcribe N+1 times within the window and assert 429 on the over-the-cap call; run `rg 'NEXT_PUBLIC_(FOREMAN_API_KEY|ANTHROPIC|OPENAI|SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE|SUPABASE_URL)' web/` and confirm zero matches in real code (test fixtures don't count). Finally update SECURITY.md to reflect what landed on this branch (no aspirational claims). Run `cd backend && npm test` and `cd web && npm test` -- they must pass. Push your branch and self-report `## Verification: unit-test-verified` in the handoff. If you run out of time, ship whatever is done with `Status: partial` and list the unfinished slices under `## Notes`.
acceptance: CEO DoD #3 evidenced: gitleaks shows zero high-severity findings on HEAD; gitleaks workflow exists at `.github/workflows/gitleaks.yml` and runs on push + PR.
Production boot test asserts the backend refuses to start without FOREMAN_API_KEY or CORS_ORIGINS (test in backend/src that runs via `cd backend && npm test`).
Vercel origin-gate test asserts `web/middleware.ts` blocks `/api/*` calls lacking a valid app origin.
Rate-limit test proves /analyse, /transcribe, /voice return 429 after the per-IP cap.
Repo grep `rg 'NEXT_PUBLIC_' web/` shows no AI/Supabase key references.
SECURITY.md updated to reflect current code on this branch (no aspirational claims).
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `git checkout <ciso-security branch>` and `npm install`.
- Install gitleaks: `curl -sSfL https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks/releases/download/v8.18.4/gitleaks_8.18.4_linux_x64.tar.gz | tar -xz -C /tmp && sudo mv /tmp/gitleaks /usr/local/bin/` (or use go install).

## Automated
- `gitleaks detect --no-banner --redact -s . --log-level info` -> zero high findings.
- `cd backend && npm test` (security tests must be among the passing).
- `rg 'NEXT_PUBLIC_(FOREMAN_API_KEY|ANTHROPIC|OPENAI|SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE)' web/` -> no matches.

## Manual
- Read SECURITY.md and confirm every claim is backed by code on this branch.

tasks.name: sre-platform
tasks.scopedGoal: You are the SRE. Own CEO DoD #1 (public URLs healthy), #5 (cost guards), and #6 (observability). Decompose into 2-3 broad workers + a verify-sre-platform verifier. Suggested workers: (W1) `ready-and-logging` = expand the /ready endpoint to return `{ ok, anthropic, openai, supabase, elevenlabs?, transcription }` booleans (new file at backend/src/routes/ready.ts is OK -- you own this slug) + structured JSON logger in production (Fastify already supports this; wire it in backend/src/index.ts) + every 5xx response carries a server-side correlation id; (W2) `cost-guards-and-payload` = per-route rate limits on /analyse, /transcribe, /voice with sensible caps + a server-side frame-size cap enforced before the analyse handler runs + billing-alert recipe documented in SECURITY.md/DEPLOY.md (the operator action, not the actual alert setup); (W3) `health-and-deploy-docs` = scripts/check-ready.sh covers the new /ready shape + DEPLOY.md documents where to read Render + Vercel logs + render.yaml and vercel.json health probes use /health (not /ready, since /ready is feature-availability not liveness). Each child MUST carry its own engineering DoD in `acceptance`. SHIP-FAST: commit and push a minimal plan.json within the first 10 minutes. MODEL CONSTRAINTS: every child MUST set `tasks[].model` to one of `claude-opus-4-7`, `gpt-5.3-codex-high-fast`, or `composer-2-fast`. Do NOT open PRs from inside the subtree.
acceptance: CEO DoD #1 evidenced: `bash scripts/check-ready.sh` on the handoff branch reports both Vercel and Render production URLs healthy.

CEO DoD #6 evidenced: `/ready` returns a JSON object with anthropic/openai/supabase booleans (and elevenlabs if configured); Fastify logger emits structured JSON in production; 5xx error responses log a correlation id; DEPLOY.md documents where to read Render + Vercel logs.

CEO DoD #5 evidenced: per-route rate limits on /analyse, /transcribe, /voice are documented in DEPLOY.md/SECURITY.md (CISO owns the test); frame-size cap enforced server-side; billing-alert recipe added to operator docs.
`npm run build --workspace backend` clean; `cd backend && npm test` passes.
scripts/check-ready.sh runs end-to-end without structural errors.

tasks.name: qa-release
tasks.scopedGoal: You are the QA Release lead. Own CEO DoD #7 (tests + readiness scripts pass + smoke E2E). Decompose into 2-3 broad workers + a verify-qa-release verifier. Suggested workers: (W1) `e2e-smoke-script` = author `scripts/smoke-e2e.sh` (or `scripts/smoke-e2e.ts` via tsx) that walks /sessions/start -> /analyse with a tiny fixture image -> /transcribe with a tiny fixture audio clip -> /sessions/:id/stop against `${BASE_URL:-http://127.0.0.1:8080}` using `${FOREMAN_API_KEY}`, exits non-zero on any failure, and prints a concise pass/fail summary; (W2) `test-suite-green` = ensure `cd backend && npm test` and `cd web && npm test` both pass on this branch (fix flakes you can fix; document anything you cannot); (W3) `release-recipe` = npm script entry like `npm run smoke` wires the smoke script in package.json, and a one-paragraph release recipe is appended to DEPLOY.md (under "Release smoke"). Each child MUST carry its own engineering DoD in `acceptance`. SHIP-FAST: commit and push a minimal plan.json within the first 10 minutes. MODEL CONSTRAINTS: every child MUST set `tasks[].model` to one of `claude-opus-4-7`, `gpt-5.3-codex-high-fast`, or `composer-2-fast`. Do NOT open PRs from inside the subtree.
acceptance: CEO DoD #7 evidenced: `scripts/smoke-e2e.*` exists and exits non-zero on any failure when run against `${BASE_URL:-http://127.0.0.1:8080}`.
`npm run build` (clean), `cd backend && npm test` (passes), `cd web && npm test` (passes) all green on your handoff branch.
`npm run smoke` (or equivalent) defined in `package.json` invokes the smoke script.

tasks.name: docs-truth-sync
tasks.scopedGoal: Own CEO DoD #8 (docs match reality). All five C-suite subplanners have handed off before you spawn -- their handoffs are pasted into your prompt as upstream context (see `## Upstream handoffs in downstream prompts`). For each of README.md, DEPLOY.md, PHONE_DEMO.md, SECURITY.md, YOUR_ACTIONS.md: (1) read the file end-to-end; (2) cross-reference each substantive claim against the upstream handoff summaries (consent UX from CPO, backend behaviour + iOS from CTO, security posture from CISO, /ready + cost guards + log docs from SRE, smoke script + test recipe from QA); (3) fix or mark `<!-- TODO: <one-line reason> -->` every claim that is not backed by code described in the handoffs. Render and Vercel URLs must be correct (`https://foreman-phi.vercel.app`, `https://foreman-api-y31r.onrender.com`). The Render free-tier cold-start caveat must be present where deployment / first-request behaviour is discussed. Do NOT modify code outside the doc files in pathsAllowed -- if a doc claim conflicts with the actual implementation, prefer marking TODO over silently editing code. Your handoff `## What I did` MUST list each doc file you touched with a one-line summary of the change.
acceptance: Each of README.md, DEPLOY.md, PHONE_DEMO.md, SECURITY.md, YOUR_ACTIONS.md has been read end-to-end and edited or annotated so every substantive claim is either (a) backed by upstream handoff evidence or (b) marked `<!-- TODO: <reason> -->`.
Render + Vercel URLs are correct in every doc.
Render free-tier cold-start caveat present where first-request behaviour is discussed.
No file outside `pathsAllowed` was modified (verify via `git diff --name-only main..<your-branch>`).

tasks.name: verify-docs-truth-sync
tasks.scopedGoal: Verify the docs pass. For each of README.md, DEPLOY.md, PHONE_DEMO.md, SECURITY.md, YOUR_ACTIONS.md, sample claims at random and check they are either backed by upstream handoff evidence (visible in your prompt as upstream context) or marked `<!-- TODO -->`. Flag any aspirational claim that escaped both. Use `## Verification: type-check-only` only if you could not run anything beyond reading the files; prefer `unit-test-verified` when you could grep against upstream handoff bodies for claim corroboration. Do NOT verify the underlying code -- the C-suite verifiers already covered that. Your job is solely to detect documentation drift.
acceptance: Verification section lists, per doc file, sampled claims + their backing (or `<!-- TODO -->`).
Any unmarked aspirational claim is flagged as a `(high)` finding.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- Inherits docs-truth-sync branch (auto-wired).

## Manual
- Read each doc file end-to-end.
- Sample 3-5 substantive claims per file and check upstream handoff bodies (in your prompt) for backing.
- Flag any unbacked unmarked claim as `(high)`.

tasks.name: merge-production-ready
tasks.scopedGoal: Merge worker. You start from `main` (default). Sequence: (1) Read the `## Branch` line in each of the SIX upstream handoffs pasted into your prompt (cpo-product-ux, cto-engineering, ciso-security, sre-platform, qa-release, docs-truth-sync) -- those are the AUTHORITATIVE branch names; do NOT assume a placeholder shape. (2) `git fetch origin <branch>:<branch>` for each upstream branch so local refs exist. (3) Merge in this order with conflict policy as noted: `git merge --no-edit <docs-truth-sync-branch>` first (docs as the canonical text baseline); then `git merge --no-edit <cpo-product-ux-branch>` (on conflict in web/components prefer CPO; in doc files prefer ours i.e. docs); then `git merge --no-edit <cto-engineering-branch>` (on conflict prefer CTO except in doc files where prefer ours); then `git merge --no-edit <ciso-security-branch>` (on conflict prefer CISO except in doc files); then `git merge --no-edit <sre-platform-branch>` (on conflict prefer SRE except in doc files); finally `git merge --no-edit <qa-release-branch>` (on conflict prefer QA; for package.json, prefer a union merge that preserves scripts from both sides). (4) After all merges complete, run `npm install`, `npm run build`, `cd backend && npm test`, `cd web && npm test`. (5) If any of those fail, attempt one round of targeted fixes (you may modify any file -- pathsAllowed is intentionally permissive for the merge worker). (6) Commit and push your branch (the cloud agent's auto-assigned branch). Your handoff MUST include a `## Measurements` block reporting the test counts and a `## What I did` listing the merge order + any conflict resolutions + any test fixes.
acceptance: All six upstream branches (`cpo-product-ux`, `cto-engineering`, `ciso-security`, `sre-platform`, `qa-release`, `docs-truth-sync`) merged into your branch, with both parents visible in `git log --merges`.
`npm run build` clean on your final branch.
`cd backend && npm test` and `cd web && npm test` both pass on your final branch.
Branch pushed and named in `## Branch` line of your handoff.
tasks.verify:

- `git checkout <merge-production-ready branch>` and `npm install`.

## Automated
- `npm run build` (clean).
- `cd backend && npm test` (passes).
- `cd web && npm test` (passes).
- `git log --merges --oneline | head -10` -> at least 5 merge commits.

## Manual
- `git diff --stat main..<your-branch>` -> changes span backend, web, shared, scripts, docs, native (a holistic delivery).

tasks.name: verify-ceo-product-readiness
tasks.scopedGoal: Verify that the merged deliverable branch satisfies every CEO DoD item in the parent plan's `acceptanceCriteria` (9 bullets). Run the full verification recipe in `verify`. For each CEO DoD bullet, record explicit pass / not met / n/a with the evidence command and its outcome. Cross-check that any DoD the merge worker (or earlier C-suite handoffs) marked as blocked-by-human-action is genuinely a human-only step (e.g. needs a third-party secret rotation), and not engineering work that was skipped. Output your verdict as `## Verification` per the verifier prompt: prefer `live-ui-verified` only if you actually exercised the deployed phone web UI end-to-end; `unit-test-verified` if you ran the smoke script + unit tests; downgrade to `type-check-only` if the build was the only signal. If a DoD item is impossible to verify from inside the cloud-agent VM (e.g. iPhone hardware capture), mark it `n/a` and explain.
acceptance: Verification section lists evidence for every CEO DoD bullet in the parent plan's `acceptanceCriteria` (9 items).
If any DoD was marked blocked-by-human-action, the verifier independently agreed (with one-line reasoning) or escalated by adding a `(high)` finding.
`## Verification` line uses one of the prescribed values.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- Inherits merge-production-ready branch (auto-wired).
- `npm install` at repo root.

## Automated
- `npm run build`.
- `cd backend && npm test` and `cd web && npm test`.
- `bash scripts/check-ready.sh` (note env gaps but do not treat missing local API keys as failure if the production URLs verify).
- Run `scripts/smoke-e2e.*` against the production stack with `BASE_URL=https://foreman-phi.vercel.app/api FOREMAN_API_KEY=<test-key-if-provided>` -- if no test key is available, point at a locally booted backend instead and document.
- `gitleaks detect --no-banner --redact -s .` -> zero high findings.

## Manual / curl
- `curl -s https://foreman-phi.vercel.app/api/health` -> `{"status":"ok"}`.
- `curl -s https://foreman-api-y31r.onrender.com/health` -> `{"status":"ok"}`.
- `curl -s https://foreman-phi.vercel.app/api/voice/config` includes `liveAvailable:true`.
- Read README/DEPLOY/SECURITY/PHONE_DEMO/YOUR_ACTIONS -- flag any aspirational claim that escaped docs-truth-sync.

## CEO DoD checklist (verifier MUST report per bullet)
For each of the 9 acceptanceCriteria bullets on the parent plan, write `- [x] <criterion text>: <evidence>` or `- [ ] <criterion text>: <reason not met>`.

## Gotchas
- The dispatcher's human operator owns secret rotation; do not block on missing ANTHROPIC/OPENAI/Supabase keys -- mark such items as `(verifier-blocked, awaiting human secret rotation)` instead of `(verifier-failed)` and explain.
- iPhone hardware smoke is `n/a` from inside this VM; rely on production URL responses + the smoke script.
